senses.definition: a wooden platform that is located in a garden on which one can put food for birds

examples.x: I placed the bird table in the garden to attract more wildlife.
The bird table is full of seeds, and the birds are enjoying their meal.
We built a wooden bird table to help feed the birds during winter.
The squirrels keep stealing food from the bird table, so I had to find a way to keep them away.
She loves watching the different species of birds visit the bird table every morning.

senses.definition: a long and narrow bed of soil on which flowers, bushes, etc. can be planted
a place in a garden where leaves, plants, etc. are stored, so that they can be turned to compost after they decay

examples.x: The gardener added kitchen scraps to the compost pile to reduce waste and create rich soil for the garden.
The compost pile in the corner of the yard was filled with food waste, grass clippings, and coffee grounds.
After raking the leaves, he threw them into the compost pile to help them break down naturally.
We need to turn the compost pile regularly to make sure it decomposes evenly.
She always adds vegetable peels and coffee filters to the compost pile to help nourish her plants.

senses.definition: a container, made of plastic or clay, in which one can grow a wide variety of plants

examples.x: She placed the new flowers in a colorful flowerpot on the windowsill.
The flowerpot on the patio was filled with vibrant geraniums.
He forgot to water the flowerpot, and the plants started to wilt.
The flowerpot cracked after being left outside during the winter.
I need to buy a larger flowerpot for my growing tomato plant.

senses.definition: an area of ground planted with flowers or plants, often arranged in a decorative or artistic pattern

examples.x: The flower bed in the front yard is full of bright daisies and tulips.
She spent the afternoon planting new flowers in the flower bed by the porch.
He added mulch to the flower bed to help the flowers grow healthier.
The garden looks beautiful, with a colorful flower bed at the center.
I love to sit on the bench and enjoy the view of the flower bed in the garden.

senses.definition: a small device with holes that is put on the end of a hose, used for watering soil or lawns; a piece of equipment that is utilized in case a building is on fire

examples.x: I turned on the sprinkler to water the garden while I was inside.
The sprinkler covered the entire lawn, ensuring every part got watered.
We need to fix the broken sprinkler before the grass gets too dry.
The kids had fun running through the sprinkler on a hot summer day.
She adjusted the sprinkler so it could reach the far corner of the yard.

senses.definition: a large woody plant with several main stems emerging from the ground
a defined area of soil, typically raised and enclosed, that is used for planting and growing plants

examples.x: She planted a variety of flowers in her garden bed to add color to the yard.
The garden bed was full of ripe tomatoes and lettuce ready for harvest.
He spent the afternoon weeding the garden bed to keep it tidy and healthy.
The garden bed was surrounded by a wooden border to keep the soil in place.
Before the frost, they covered the garden bed with a protective cloth to keep the plants safe.

senses.definition: a portable vessel with a long tube and a handle, used for sprinkling water on plants

examples.x: She filled the watering can with water and carefully watered the flowers in the garden.
The children took turns using the watering can to help their mother in the garden.
He used a small watering can to water the plants on the balcony.
After planting the seeds, she grabbed the watering can to give them a good soak.
I bought a new watering can because the old one was leaking.

senses.definition: a structure, often placed in a pool or lake, that pumps a long, narrow stream of water up into the air for decorative purposes

examples.x: The fountain in the park was surrounded by colorful flowers.
Coins glistened at the bottom of the wishing fountain.
The fountain sprayed water high into the air, cooling the area.
They took pictures by the beautiful marble fountain in the square.
The fountain in the garden added a peaceful ambiance.

senses.definition: a decorative stone or plastic figure of a dwarfish creature with a pointed hat
an automated network of pipes and sprinkler heads used to water plants and vegetation in a designated area

examples.x: The sprinkler system in the garden automatically waters the flowers every morning.
He checked the sprinkler system to make sure all the heads were working properly.
The sprinkler system broke down, so we had to water the garden by hand for a week.
We installed a sprinkler system to keep our lawn green during the dry summer months.

senses.definition: a raised area in a garden surrounded by a wooden or stone frame, used for growing plants in

examples.x: She decided to plant vegetables in a raised bed to make gardening easier on her back.
The raised bed helped the plants grow better by allowing the soil to drain properly.
The garden looks much tidier now that the flowers are planted in raised beds.
They filled the raised bed with rich compost to give the tomatoes the best start.
He built a raised bed using wooden planks to keep the soil contained and organized.

senses.definition: any organism, such as insects, rodents, or other animals, that causes damage or harm to plants or crops in a garden or agricultural setting

examples.x: The garden pest was eating the leaves of the tomato plants, leaving them damaged.
To protect the flowers, she used a natural spray to keep the garden pests away.
The rabbits were a common garden pest, nibbling on the newly planted vegetables.
Organic gardeners prefer using natural methods to get rid of garden pests instead of chemicals.
The gardener planted marigolds to keep garden pests from attacking the vegetable patch.

senses.definition: a long, thin, pointed rod, usually made of wood or metal, that is used to support and train plants, such as tomato plants or climbing vines, to grow in a certain direction or stay upright

examples.x: The gardener used a garden stake to support the young tomato plant.
She placed a garden stake next to each flower to help them grow tall and straight.
The wind had knocked over the delicate plants, so he added a few garden stakes to keep them upright.
I need to buy garden stakes to secure the climbing roses to the trellis.
The garden stakes helped prevent the heavy sunflowers from bending under their own weight.

senses.definition: grass-covered area of ground, often used for lawns or fields

examples.x: The children played soccer on the lush green grass.
We had a picnic on the soft grass in the park.
They decided to sit on the grass by the river.
He lay on the grass to enjoy the sunny weather.
The dog rolled around in the grass after running in circles.

senses.definition: a mix of sand and small pebbles that cover the surface of some roads
a row of closely-planted bushes or small trees that form a boundary, particularly on the edge of a garden, road, or field

examples.x: The garden was enclosed by a tall hedge that provided privacy.
They trimmed the hedge along the driveway to keep it neat.
He planted a hedge around the backyard to create a natural fence.
The rabbits often hid behind the hedge when they played in the garden.
A low hedge separated the two front yards, allowing for visibility and easy access.

senses.definition: an area, often in a garden, where a large number of shrubs are thickly planted
an artificial pond or a fountain that is placed in some parks or gardens to provide decoration

examples.x: They installed a water feature near the entrance to make the yard feel more inviting.
A water feature, like a pond with lilies, can add a touch of elegance to any garden.
The landscaper suggested adding a water feature to attract birds and butterflies to the yard.
The park’s new water feature includes a gentle waterfall surrounded by flowers and trees.

senses.definition: a small, man-made structure designed to provide a nesting place for birds

examples.x: She hung a birdhouse on the tree in her backyard to attract more birds.
The children enjoyed watching the birds fly in and out of the birdhouse.
He built a birdhouse using wood from the old fence in the garden.
We saw a robin peeking out of the birdhouse this morning.
The birdhouse needed cleaning after the birds had flown south for the winter.

senses.definition: a lengthy, flexible tube, made of plastic or rubber, often used in firefighting and gardening, that can direct the flow of water to the direction it is pointed at
an area of grass, typically in a yard or garden, that is cut and maintained at a short length

examples.x: They spent the afternoon mowing the lawn to keep it looking neat and well-kept.
The children played games on the large, green lawn in the backyard.
The community park featured a spacious lawn perfect for picnics and outdoor activities.
She planted colorful flowers along the edge of the lawn to add visual interest.
The lawn was carefully landscaped with decorative shrubs and trees for an attractive appearance.

senses.definition: a man-made structure that provides a safe habitat for solitary bee species to nest

examples.x: The garden was buzzing with activity after I placed a bee house near the flowers.
The carpenter built a beautiful bee house that became a favorite spot for local bees.
To encourage more pollinators, she added a bee house to her backyard.
The bee house, made of bamboo tubes, attracted several solitary bees.
We noticed more bees around the garden after we installed a bee house by the fence.

senses.definition: a decorative object or structure that is placed in a garden or outdoor area to enhance its aesthetic appeal

examples.x: The garden ornament in the shape of a butterfly added a touch of whimsy to the backyard.
She placed a stone frog as a garden ornament near the flower bed to brighten the area.
The colorful wind chimes were a popular garden ornament that made pleasant sounds when the wind blew.
They added a small fairy garden ornament beside the rose bush to create a magical atmosphere.
He decided to buy a birdbath as a garden ornament to attract more birds to his garden.

senses.definition: a large outdoor container that can collect and store rainwater running off rooftops

examples.x: The gardener uses a rain barrel to water the plants during the dry months.
We installed a rain barrel to collect water for the garden and reduce our water usage.
The rain barrel overflowed after several days of heavy rain.
They decided to buy a rain barrel to make their garden more sustainable.

senses.definition: a container placed in an outdoor garden for storing organic waste so it can be turned to compost later

examples.x: He placed the food scraps in the compost bin to create nutrient-rich soil for his garden.
The compost bin in the backyard helps reduce the amount of waste sent to the landfill.
After trimming the bushes, she added the yard waste to the compost bin.
By using a compost bin, they were able to create healthy soil for their flower beds.

senses.definition: an area of land where edible plants, such as vegetables and herbs, are grown for personal use or sale

examples.x: She spent the afternoon planting new crops in her vegetable garden.
After weeks of work, their vegetable garden was full of ripe tomatoes and peppers.
We built a small vegetable garden on the rooftop to grow some easy-to-care-for plants.
The children loved picking fresh carrots and cucumbers from their vegetable garden.
Every weekend, he waters the vegetable garden and checks on the growth of his plants.

senses.definition: a slender, vertical wooden piece used as one of the upright components of a fence

examples.x: The old gate was made of wooden pickets, worn and weathered by time.
He replaced the broken picket in the fence to restore its neat appearance.
A missing picket left a gap in the otherwise pristine boundary of the yard.
The children painted each picket of the fence a different color, creating a vibrant and playful look.
The picket was loose, and it creaked whenever the wind blew through the yard.

senses.definition: a sturdy pole made of metal or timber that is dug into the ground to be used as a marker or support something
a framework made of metal or wood bars for supporting climbing plants or fruit trees
a plumbing fixture designed for use outdoors, typically found in gardens, near pools, or in beach houses, that allows people to shower outside, often using natural light and air

examples.x: On hot summer days, she enjoys using the outdoor shower to cool off.
The garden had an outdoor shower, perfect for cleaning up after gardening.
We installed an outdoor shower by the pool to make it easy for guests to rinse off.
The outdoor shower is surrounded by tall plants, offering privacy while you bathe.
They added an outdoor shower to their patio to make outdoor living more enjoyable.

senses.definition: a small structure typically located in a backyard or garden area, used for storing gardening tools, equipment, supplies, and other items related to gardening or outdoor activities

examples.x: He stored all his gardening tools in the garden shed.
The garden shed was filled with bags of soil, pots, and watering cans.
She spent the afternoon painting the inside of the garden shed.
The children love to play in the garden shed, pretending it is a secret clubhouse.
After trimming the bushes, I put the hedge clippers back in the garden shed.

senses.definition: a shelter in a park or garden, that is surrounded by plants, such as climbing shrubs or vines, that people can sit under and relax

examples.x: The backyard featured an arbor covered in wisteria that provided a shady nook for enjoying morning coffee.
We set up tables and chairs under the arbor for the outdoor wedding reception after the ceremony.
Grapevines climbed all over the arbor at the end of the driveway, signaling visitors had arrived at the farmhouse.
